title: R package {dagsterpipes}
text: R package implements Dagster Pipes protocol, enabling R scripts run external processes orchestrated Dagster. communicates bidirectionally Dagster: receiving execution context (asset keys, partition info, extras) reporting back materializations, asset checks, logs, custom messages.

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
text: Minimal dependencies: {R6} {jsonlite} allowed Imports dependencies. functionality must use base R ({httr}, {curl}, etc.). Single transport: File-based message writing (newline-delimited JSON appended temp file). matches default PipesSubprocessClient / PipesTempFileMessageReader Dagster side. Graceful degradation: DAGSTER_PIPES_CONTEXT set (.e., script run outside Dagster), open_dagster_pipes() returns -op context logs console silently ignores materializations. allows scripts tested standalone. R6 class system: Use R6 stateful objects (PipesContext, message writer channel).

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
text: R: Use jsonlite::base64_dec() base64 decoding, base::memDecompress() zlib decompression, jsonlite::fromJSON() JSON parsing.
code: env_var_value -> base64_decode -> zlib_decompress -> JSON_parse -> {"path": "/tmp/xxx"} decode_param <- function(encoded) {   raw_bytes <- jsonlite::base64_dec(encoded)               # {jsonlite}   decompressed <- memDecompress(raw_bytes, type = "gzip")  # base R   jsonlite::fromJSON(rawToChar(decompressed), simplifyVector = FALSE) }

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
text: decode_param(env_var_value) — Full decode pipeline: jsonlite::base64_dec() -> memDecompress() -> jsonlite::fromJSON() json_serialize(obj) — Thin wrapper around jsonlite::toJSON() appropriate defaults (auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null", digits = NA) produce Dagster-compatible JSON

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
code: PipesContext <- R6::R6Class("PipesContext",   public = list(     initialize = function(context_data, message_channel) { ... },      # Properties (active bindings)     # asset_keys, asset_key, run_id, job_name, retry_number,     # extras, partition_key, partition_key_range, partition_time_window,     # is_asset_step, is_partition_step, provenance, code_version      # Methods     get_extra = function(key) { ... },     log = function(message, level = "INFO") { ... },     report_asset_materialization = function(metadata = NULL, asset_key = NULL, data_version = NULL) { ... },     report_asset_check = function(check_name, passed, asset_key = NULL, severity = "ERROR", metadata = NULL) { ... },     report_custom_message = function(payload) { ... },     close = function() { ... }   ),   active = list(     asset_keys = function() private$.context_data$asset_keys,     asset_key = function() { ... },  # error if length != 1     run_id = function() private$.context_data$run_id,     job_name = function() private$.context_data$job_name,     retry_number = function() private$.context_data$retry_number,     extras = function() private$.context_data$extras,     partition_key = function() private$.context_data$partition_key,     is_asset_step = function() length(private$.context_data$asset_keys) > 0,     is_partition_step = function() !is.null(private$.context_data$partition_key)   ),   private = list(     .context_data = NULL,     .message_channel = NULL,     .closed = FALSE,     write_message = function(method, params = NULL) { ... }   ) )

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
code: PipesFileMessageWriterChannel <- R6::R6Class("PipesFileMessageWriterChannel",   public = list(     initialize = function(path) { ... },     write_message = function(message) {       # message is a list: list(`__dagster_pipes_version` = "0.1", method = ..., params = ...)       json_line <- jsonlite::toJSON(message, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null")       cat(json_line, "\n", file = private$.path, append = TRUE, sep = "")     },     close = function() { ... }   ),   private = list(     .path = NULL   ) )

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
code: #' Open a Dagster Pipes session #' #' @return A PipesContext object (or a no-op mock if not running under Dagster) #' @export open_dagster_pipes <- function() {   context_env <- Sys.getenv("DAGSTER_PIPES_CONTEXT", unset = "")   messages_env <- Sys.getenv("DAGSTER_PIPES_MESSAGES", unset = "")    if (context_env == "" || messages_env == "") {     message("Not running under Dagster Pipes. Returning no-op context.")     return(NullPipesContext$new())   }    context_params <- decode_param(context_env)   messages_params <- decode_param(messages_env)    context_data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(context_params$path, simplifyVector = FALSE)   message_channel <- PipesFileMessageWriterChannel$new(messages_params$path)    ctx <- PipesContext$new(context_data, message_channel)   # Send "opened" message   ctx }

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
code: #' Create a typed metadata value #' @export pipes_metadata_value <- function(raw_value, type = "__infer__") {   list(raw_value = raw_value, type = type) }

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
text: lightweight R6 class mirrors PipesContext’s interface nothing. Log calls go message(), report calls silently ignored.

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
code: Package: dagsterpipes Title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R Version: 0.1.0 Authors@R: person("Joseph", "Kirincic", role = c("aut", "cre")) Description: Implements the Dagster Pipes protocol, enabling R scripts to     communicate with the Dagster orchestrator. R scripts can receive execution     context and report asset materializations, check results, and log messages     back to Dagster. License: MIT + file LICENSE Encoding: UTF-8 Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) RoxygenNote: 7.3.3 Imports: R6, jsonlite Suggests: testthat (>= 3.0.0) Config/testthat/edition: 3

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
text: Use jsonlite::fromJSON(txt, simplifyVector = FALSE) parsing context data preserve list--lists structure (avoid R auto-simplifying data frames/vectors). Use jsonlite::toJSON(x, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null") serializing messages single values wrapped arrays NULLs become JSON null. Use jsonlite::base64_dec() decoding bootstrap env var values.

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
text: Unit test decode_param (mock base64+zlib-compressed input, verify correct output) Test PipesContext mock context data temp file message channel Integration test: simulate full Dagster Pipes flow setting env vars, calling open_dagster_pipes(), reporting materialization, closing, reading messages file verify correct JSON output Test -op/null context behavior env vars absent

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
code: import dagster as dg  @dg.asset(compute_kind="R") def my_r_asset(     context: dg.AssetExecutionContext,     pipes_subprocess_client: dg.PipesSubprocessClient, ):     return pipes_subprocess_client.run(         command=["Rscript", "my_script.R"],         context=context,         extras={"threshold": 0.5, "output_path": "/tmp/results.csv"},     ).get_materialize_result()  defs = dg.Definitions(     assets=[my_r_asset],     resources={"pipes_subprocess_client": dg.PipesSubprocessClient()}, )

title: R package {dagsterpipes}
code: library(dagsterpipes)  ctx <- open_dagster_pipes()  threshold <- ctx$get_extra("threshold") output_path <- ctx$get_extra("output_path")  # ... do work ...  ctx$report_asset_materialization(   metadata = list(     row_count = pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int"),     output_path = pipes_metadata_value(output_path, "path")   ) )  ctx$log("Processing complete", level = "INFO") ctx$close()

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Dagster Python-based orchestrator data pipelines. Dagster Pipes protocol uses run external processes (language) part pipeline, still collecting structured information — asset materializations, asset checks, logs, metadata — back processes. dagsterpipes implements Dagster Pipes Protocol R language. lets write ordinary R scripts Dagster can launch subprocesses, receive execution context Dagster (asset keys, partition info, user-supplied “extras”), report results back way Dagster understands natively. high level, workflow : Dagster asset Python calls PipesSubprocessClient.run() command like Rscript my_script.R. Dagster launches R subprocess, injecting two environment variables (DAGSTER_PIPES_CONTEXT DAGSTER_PIPES_MESSAGES) point two temp files, one R script reads one writes . R script calls with_dagster_pipes(), reads context file, runs user-supplied function PipesContext, closes session automatically (forwarding error Dagster). Inside function, R script work, logs, reports materialization. Dagster tails messages file turns line JSON corresponding Dagster event (AssetMaterialization, AssetCheckResult, log entry, etc.). use ? team R-based models, scoring pipelines, reporting jobs want schedule monitor alongside Python assets — without porting R code Python wrapping opaque shell step — Pipes tool .

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Dagster passes two environment variables R subprocess: context file contains JSON object asset keys step, run ID, job name, partition info, extras Python asset supplied. messages file newline-delimited JSON. Every message R process writes single line shaped like: Pictorially: don’t deal encoding, decoding, file /O : with_dagster_pipes() PipesContext methods handle .
code: {"__dagster_pipes_version": "0.1", "method": "<name>", "params": {...}} Python (Dagster)                                R subprocess     ----------------                                ------------     PipesSubprocessClient.run()          |          | sets env vars,          | spawns Rscript          v                                              Rscript my_script.R                                                    |                                                    | with_dagster_pipes()                                                    v                                            reads <context file>                                                    |                                                    | ctx$log(...)                                                    | ctx$report_asset_materialization(...)                                                    | ctx$close()                                                    v                                           appends to <messages file>          ^          | tails messages file,          | turns lines into Dagster events          |     AssetMaterialization / logs / checks

what: Writing your first R Pipes script
title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Let’s consider simple example. Save my_script.R root Dagster project: things worth noting: with_dagster_pipes() opens session, runs function, closes — including forwarding error Dagster (see Handling errors ). opened message sent automatically session starts; closed message sent function returns (throws). ctx$log() accepts levels "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL". mapped Dagster’s log levels.
code: library(dagsterpipes)  with_dagster_pipes(function(ctx) {   # Read inputs supplied by the Python asset via `extras=...`   threshold   <- ctx$get_extra("threshold")   output_path <- ctx$get_extra("output_path")    ctx$log(     sprintf("Starting run with threshold = %s", threshold),     level = "INFO"   )    # --- do real work here ---   df <- data.frame(x = runif(1000), y = runif(1000))   df <- df[df$x > threshold, ]   write.csv(df, output_path, row.names = FALSE)    # Report the materialization with typed metadata   ctx$report_asset_materialization(     metadata = list(       row_count   = pipes_metadata_value(nrow(df), "int"),       output_path = pipes_metadata_value(output_path, "path"),       threshold   = pipes_metadata_value(threshold, "float")     )   )    ctx$log("Done", level = "INFO") })

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Dagster side, define asset runs script via PipesSubprocessClient: extras dict shows R side via ctx$get_extra("..."). asset key comes Python decorator (my_r_asset ), can access R side ctx$asset_key.
code: import dagster as dg  @dg.asset(compute_kind="R") def my_r_asset(     context: dg.AssetExecutionContext,     pipes_subprocess_client: dg.PipesSubprocessClient, ):     return pipes_subprocess_client.run(         command=["Rscript", "my_script.R"],         context=context,         extras={             "threshold": 0.5,             "output_path": "/tmp/results.csv",         },     ).get_materialize_result()  defs = dg.Definitions(     assets=[my_r_asset],     resources={"pipes_subprocess_client": dg.PipesSubprocessClient()}, )

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Assuming Python code lives defs.py my_script.R path Dagster can see, start Dagster locally: Open Dagster UI (usually http://localhost:3000), navigate asset my_r_asset, click Materialize. Dagster : Spawn Rscript my_script.R subprocess. Inject two Pipes env vars. Stream log lines materialization event back UI R process writes . ’ll see AssetMaterialization metadata reported, plus ctx$log() messages log viewer.

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Dagster asset checks assessing quality assets. want run asset checks using R, dagsterpipes covered. Just use report_asset_check(): Python side, declare check @dg.asset_check via check_specs= argument asset, Dagster match check_name report declared spec.
code: ctx$report_asset_check(   check_name = "row_count_above_zero",   passed     = nrow(df) > 0,   severity   = "ERROR",   metadata   = list(     row_count = pipes_metadata_value(nrow(df), "int")   ) )

what: Sending a custom payload back to Dagster
title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Sometimes need return value R subprocess doesn’t fit materialization check — model object summary, row results pass downstream Dagster asset, arbitrary structured payload. ctx$report_custom_message() sends free-form JSON-serializable payload back Dagster: payload can R value jsonlite::toJSON() can serialize — scalar, named list, nested structure. Python side, Dagster asset can collect messages passing custom_message_handler PipesSubprocessClient.run(), reading PipesClientCompletedInvocation returned .run(). example: Use report_custom_message() need pass something structured R subprocess calling Python asset isn’t first-class Dagster event. typed metadata appear materialization , use metadata argument report_asset_materialization() instead.
code: ctx$report_custom_message(   payload = list(     model       = "glm",     coefficients = list(intercept = 0.12, slope = 0.87),     auc         = 0.91,     n_train     = 10000L   ) ) @dg.asset(compute_kind="R") def my_r_asset(     context: dg.AssetExecutionContext,     pipes_subprocess_client: dg.PipesSubprocessClient, ):     result = pipes_subprocess_client.run(         command=["Rscript", "my_script.R"],         context=context,     )     custom_messages = result.get_custom_messages()     # custom_messages is a list of the payloads sent from R     return result.get_materialize_result()

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: R code throws, want Dagster know — otherwise subprocess exits non-zero Dagster just sees “process failed” structured error. with_dagster_pipes() handles automatically. function pass throws error, exception forwarded Dagster via closed message (including error message, class, formatted stack), error re-raised R process still exits non-zero: need control session lifecycle, can use open_dagster_pipes() directly manage error handling :
code: with_dagster_pipes(function(ctx) {   threshold <- ctx$get_extra("threshold")   # ... do work that may fail ...   ctx$report_asset_materialization(metadata = list()) }) # If the function errors, Dagster sees the structured exception automatically. ctx <- open_dagster_pipes()  tryCatch({   threshold <- ctx$get_extra("threshold")   # ... do work that may fail ...   ctx$report_asset_materialization(metadata = list())   ctx$close() }, error = function(e) {   ctx$close(exception = e)   stop(e) })

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: Metadata values attached materializations checks typed. Use pipes_metadata_value(raw_value, type) tag value specific type, let Dagster infer omitting type argument (default "__infer__"). examples can evaluate directly:
code: library(dagsterpipes)  pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int") #> $raw_value #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $type #> [1] "int" pipes_metadata_value("/tmp/out.csv", "path") #> $raw_value #> [1] "/tmp/out.csv" #>  #> $type #> [1] "path" pipes_metadata_value("https://example.com/run/42", "url") #> $raw_value #> [1] "https://example.com/run/42" #>  #> $type #> [1] "url" pipes_metadata_value(0.97, "float") #> $raw_value #> [1] 0.97 #>  #> $type #> [1] "float"  # Default: let Dagster infer pipes_metadata_value("hello world") #> $raw_value #> [1] "hello world" #>  #> $type #> [1] "__infer__"

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: ctx$report_asset_materialization() ctx$report_asset_check() normalize metadata argument: value already pipes_metadata_value() result (list exactly raw_value type), ’s auto-wrapped type = "__infer__". two forms equivalent: Use explicit form want specific rendering UI (e.g. "md", "path", "url") inferred type wrong.
code: # Explicit ctx$report_asset_materialization(   metadata = list(     row_count = pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int"),     note      = pipes_metadata_value("ok", "text")   ) )  # Shorthand (types inferred by Dagster) ctx$report_asset_materialization(   metadata = list(     row_count = 1000L,     note      = "ok"   ) )

title: Getting started with dagsterpipes
text: common pain point frameworks like can’t easily run script laptop development — expects env vars orchestrator isn’t running. dagsterpipes handles returning NullPipesContext DAGSTER_PIPES_CONTEXT DAGSTER_PIPES_MESSAGES aren’t set. NullPipesContext interface PipesContext, : log() calls go message() (.e., R console / stderr). report_asset_materialization(), report_asset_check(), report_custom_message() silent -ops. get_extra() returns NULL (prints notice). Context fields like asset_keys, run_id, partition_key return sensible empty values. means script works inside Dagster plain Rscript my_script.R interactive session. can try right now: log line shows console, materialization ignored, can iterate script without Dagster . ’re happy , hand file PipesSubprocessClient “light ” full orchestration.
code: library(dagsterpipes)  with_dagster_pipes(function(ctx) {   ctx$log("running standalone", level = "INFO")   ctx$report_asset_materialization(     metadata = list(row_count = pipes_metadata_value(42L, "int"))   ) }) #> Not running under Dagster Pipes. Returning no-op context. #> [INFO] running standalone

title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R

title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R
text: dagsterpipes implements Dagster Pipes protocol R. lets R scripts receive execution context Dagster (asset keys, partitions, extras) report materializations, asset checks, logs, custom messages back orchestrator. R counterpart Python’s dagster-pipes JavaScript reference implementation.

title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R
text: Install GitHub either remotes pak:

title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R
text: Python / Dagster side, define asset runs R script via PipesSubprocessClient: R side (my_script.R), use with_dagster_pipes() open session, work, report back. session closed automatically — including forwarding error Dagster:
code: import dagster as dg  @dg.asset(compute_kind="R") def my_r_asset(     context: dg.AssetExecutionContext,     pipes_subprocess_client: dg.PipesSubprocessClient, ):     return pipes_subprocess_client.run(         command=["Rscript", "my_script.R"],         context=context,         extras={"threshold": 0.5, "output_path": "/tmp/results.csv"},     ).get_materialize_result()  defs = dg.Definitions(     assets=[my_r_asset],     resources={"pipes_subprocess_client": dg.PipesSubprocessClient()}, ) library(dagsterpipes)  with_dagster_pipes(function(ctx) {   threshold <- ctx$get_extra("threshold")   output_path <- ctx$get_extra("output_path")    # ... do work ...    ctx$report_asset_materialization(     metadata = list(       row_count   = pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int"),       output_path = pipes_metadata_value(output_path, "path")     )   )    ctx$log("Processing complete", level = "INFO") })

title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R
text: Minimal dependencies: R6 jsonlite. File-based message transport (newline-delimited JSON), matching Dagster’s default PipesSubprocessClient / PipesTempFileMessageReader. Automatic session lifecycle: with_dagster_pipes() opens, closes, forwards errors Dagster — manual tryCatch needed. Graceful degradation: Dagster env vars absent, -op NullPipesContext returned scripts can run tested outside Dagster. R6-based API active bindings context fields (asset_key, run_id, partition_key, extras, etc.).

title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R
text: DAGSTER_PIPES_CONTEXT DAGSTER_PIPES_MESSAGES set, with_dagster_pipes() (open_dagster_pipes()) returns NullPipesContext. logs R console via message() silently ignores materialization, check, custom-message reports. means script can run interactively plain Rscript invocation without Dagster machinery.

title: Dagster Pipes Protocol for R
text: Metadata values reported Dagster typed. Use pipes_metadata_value(raw_value, type) wrap value type annotation: Supported types: text, url, path, notebook, json, md, float, int, bool, dagster_run, asset, null, table, table_schema, table_column_lineage, timestamp, __infer__ (default, lets Dagster infer type raw value).

title: Pipes Context — PipesContext
text: Pipes Context Pipes Context

title: Pipes Context — PipesContext
text: Holds execution context received Dagster provides methods report materializations, asset checks, logs, custom messages back orchestrator.

title: Pipes Context — PipesContext
text: asset_keys list asset keys context. asset_key single asset key. Errors exactly one. run_id Dagster run ID. job_name Dagster job name. retry_number current retry number. extras extras dictionary passed Dagster. partition_key partition key, NULL. partition_key_range partition key range, NULL. partition_time_window partition time window, NULL. is_asset_step Whether step involves assets. is_partition_step Whether step involves partitions. provenance provenance info asset key. code_version code version info asset key.

title: Pipes Context — PipesContext
text: Close Pipes session. Optionally report exception Dagster passing error/condition object; exception serialized closed message params per Pipes protocol.

title: Pipes Context — PipesContext
text: exception Optional condition/error object. supplied, message, class, formatted stack attached closed message sent Dagster. NULL (default), clean closed message empty params sent.

title: Pipes Context — PipesContext
text: objects class cloneable method.

title: Pipes Context — PipesContext
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ctx <- open_dagster_pipes()  tryCatch({   output_path <- ctx$get_extra("output_path")   ctx$log("starting work", level = "INFO")    # ... do work that may fail ...    ctx$report_asset_materialization(     metadata = list(       row_count  = pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int"),       output_path = pipes_metadata_value(output_path, "path")     )   )   ctx$close() }, error = function(e) {   # Forward the exception to Dagster via the `closed` message.   ctx$close(exception = e)   stop(e) }) } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ctx <- open_dagster_pipes()  threshold <- ctx$get_extra("threshold") ctx$log(sprintf("Using threshold = %s", threshold), level = "INFO")  # ... do work ...  ctx$report_asset_materialization(   metadata = list(     row_count = pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int")   ) ) ctx$close() } # }

what: Create a typed metadata value for Dagster Pipes — pipes_metadata_value
title: Create a typed metadata value for Dagster Pipes — pipes_metadata_value
text: Wraps raw value type annotation Dagster Pipes protocol.

title: Create a typed metadata value for Dagster Pipes — pipes_metadata_value
Create a typed metadata value for Dagster Pipes — pipes_metadata_value
text: raw_value value report. type metadata type. One "text", "url", "path", "notebook", "json", "md", "float", "int", "bool", "dagster_run", "asset", "null", "table", "table_schema", "table_column_lineage", "timestamp", "__infer__" (default).

title: Create a typed metadata value for Dagster Pipes — pipes_metadata_value
text: list raw_value type elements.

title: Create a typed metadata value for Dagster Pipes — pipes_metadata_value
code: pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int") #> $raw_value #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $type #> [1] "int" #>  pipes_metadata_value("/data/output.csv", "path") #> $raw_value #> [1] "/data/output.csv" #>  #> $type #> [1] "path" #>  pipes_metadata_value(0.97, "float") #> $raw_value #> [1] 0.97 #>  #> $type #> [1] "float" #>  # Let Dagster infer the type from the raw value: pipes_metadata_value("hello world") #> $raw_value #> [1] "hello world" #>  #> $type #> [1] "__infer__" #>

what: Run code within a Dagster Pipes session — with_dagster_pipes
title: Run code within a Dagster Pipes session — with_dagster_pipes
text: Opens Dagster Pipes session, passes context user-supplied function, guarantees session closed afterwards. function raises error, exception forwarded Dagster re-raising.

title: Run code within a Dagster Pipes session — with_dagster_pipes
Run code within a Dagster Pipes session — with_dagster_pipes
text: code function takes single argument (PipesContext NullPipesContext) performs pipeline work.

title: Run code within a Dagster Pipes session — with_dagster_pipes
text: return value code, invisibly.

title: Run code within a Dagster Pipes session — with_dagster_pipes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ with_dagster_pipes(function(ctx) {   threshold <- ctx$get_extra("threshold")   ctx$log(sprintf("Using threshold = %s", threshold))    # ... do work ...    ctx$report_asset_materialization(     metadata = list(       row_count = pipes_metadata_value(1000L, "int")     )   ) }) } # }

title: dagsterpipes 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Implements Dagster Pipes protocol R, enabling R scripts run external processes orchestrated Dagster. Provides with_dagster_pipes(), open_dagster_pipes(), PipesContext, NullPipesContext, pipes_metadata_value() public API. File-based message transport compatible Dagster’s default PipesSubprocessClient / PipesTempFileMessageReader. Minimal dependency footprint (R6, jsonlite).
